metadata.scheme_name: Free Caste Hostel (De-Notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Communities)

raw_data.details: The "Free Caste Hostel" scheme by the Department of De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Welfare, Government of Madhya Pradesh, provides a scholarship of ₹1,230 per month for boys and ₹1,270 per month for girls for 10 months. The scheme benefits students from De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic tribes studying in local educational institutions.
raw_data.benefits: A scholarship of ₹1,230 per month for boys. A scholarship of ₹1,270 per month for girls. The scholarship is given for 10 months.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be a student belonging to the De-Notified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic tribes. The applicant should be studying in a local educational institution. The applicant should apply for admission before the new academic session. The applicant should secure a vacant seat, as new admissions depend on availability. The applicant should have a higher percentage in the last passed examination for priority admission.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineRegistration
Step 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)".
Step 2: Navigate to the top right corner of the homepage and click "[Registration".](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Registration/Default)
Step 3: On the registration page, provide the mandatory details. (the fields marked with * are mandatory). If required, verify your Mobile Number and/or Email ID via OTP. Finally, click "Register". Application
Step 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)".
Step 2: Use the credentials you created during registration to log in.
Step 3: Navigate to the online application form of the scheme you intend to apply for.
Step 4: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size.
Step 5: Carefully review all the information provided. Make any necessary corrections.
Step 6: Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions, declaration, and privacy policy (if any).
Step 7: Click the "Submit" to submit your application.
raw_data.documents_required: Passport-size PhotographAadhaar CardDomicile CertificateCaste CertificateSamagra IDBank Account Details

Documents RequiredPassport-size PhotographAadhaar CardDomicile CertificateCaste CertificateSamagra IDBank Account DetailsFrequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the Free caste hostel Scheme? The scheme provides financial assistance to students of de-notified, nomadic, and semi-nomadic tribes studying in local educational institutions.

Who is eligible for this scheme? Students belonging to de-notified, nomadic, and semi-nomadic tribes who are enrolled in local educational institutions are eligible. What is the amount of scholarship provided? The scheme provides ₹1,230 per month for boys and ₹1,270 per month for girls for 10 months.

How can students apply for this scheme? Eligible students must apply using the prescribed application form before the start of the academic session. Is there a renewal process for existing beneficiaries? Yes, renewal is automatic for resident students, and they do not need to reapply. Are there any academic requirements for admission? Admission is based on merit, with preference given to students who have secured higher marks in the previous academic year. Can new students apply if seats are full? No, new students can only apply if there are vacant seats in the hostel, ashram, or community welfare center. What documents are required for application? Applicants need to submit caste certificates, proof of enrollment in an educational institution, and previous academic records. Is there any income limit for eligibility? No, the scheme does not have an income limit for applicants. What is the duration of the scholarship? The scholarship is provided for 10 months in an academic year. Sources And References[Scheme Details](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Public/Pages/SchemeDetail? ID=1)

faqs.question: What is the Free caste hostel Scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides financial assistance to students of de-notified, nomadic, and semi-nomadic tribes studying in local educational institutions.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Students belonging to de-notified, nomadic, and semi-nomadic tribes who are enrolled in local educational institutions are eligible.

faqs.question: What is the amount of scholarship provided?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides ₹1,230 per month for boys and ₹1,270 per month for girls for 10 months.

faqs.question: How can students apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Eligible students must apply using the prescribed application form before the start of the academic session.

faqs.question: Is there a renewal process for existing beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: Yes, renewal is automatic for resident students, and they do not need to reapply.

faqs.question: Are there any academic requirements for admission?
faqs.answer: Admission is based on merit, with preference given to students who have secured higher marks in the previous academic year.

faqs.question: Can new students apply if seats are full?
faqs.answer: No, new students can only apply if there are vacant seats in the hostel, ashram, or community welfare center.

faqs.question: What documents are required for application?
faqs.answer: Applicants need to submit caste certificates, proof of enrollment in an educational institution, and previous academic records.

faqs.question: Is there any income limit for eligibility?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme does not have an income limit for applicants.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the scholarship?
faqs.answer: The scholarship is provided for 10 months in an academic year.
